conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and he's 17 years old. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is over 16, his eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether he's in approved education or training. 

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs, or certain other approved courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any of that. He's actually doing an apprenticeship at the moment - he's training to be an electrician.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. That's helpful information.

To determine Alex's eligibility, I need to know a bit more about the apprenticeship:

**Where is Alex doing his apprenticeship - is it in England, or in another part of the UK (Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's doing it here in England - we live in Birmingham.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official guidance I've reviewed, I can now provide you with a decision.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Decision: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

I'm sorry to inform you that you're not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Here's why:

Although Child Benefit can normally continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training, the rules specifically state that **Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England**.

Since Alex is 17 and has started an apprenticeship as an electrician in England, Child Benefit cannot be paid for him, even though he's under 20 years old.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, it would have stopped when he started his apprenticeship. If you have any questions about this decision or your specific circumstances, you can contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is doing an apprenticeship in England. According to the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. While Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 who stay in approved education or training, apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded from this provision. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 17, who is doing an apprenticeship in England. Under the Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, even though they are under 20. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Under the Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, even though they are under 20. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
